title: Dill-Pickle Smoked Filipino Chicken Tinola
description: A delicious, Buddhist-temple-friendly twist on traditional Filipino tinola, featuring smoky flavor from the clay pot and tangy dill pickles.
instructions: Preheat your clay pot or tajine vessel to 300°F (150°C).
Heat the coconut oil in the preheated pot over medium heat.
Add the sliced ginger and minced garlic and sauté until fragrant, about 2 minutes.
Add the chopped dill pickles and sauté for another minute.
Add the chicken, fish sauce (if using), smoked paprika, bay leaves, and black peppercorns. Stir to combine.
Pour in the chicken broth and coconut milk, and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the oven, or continue cooking over low heat on the stovetop.
Braise the chicken for 20-25 minutes, or until it's cooked through and the sauce has thickened.
Season the tinola with salt to taste.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh cilantro and steamed vegetables on the side.
